title: श्री शनि सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शनि सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥

नीलाञ्जनसमाभासं रविपुत्रं यमाग्रजम्।
छायामार्तण्डसम्भूतं तं नमामि शनैश्चरम्॥

कृष्णवर्णं चतुर्बाहुं गृध्रवाहनमारूढम्।
धनुर्बाणं त्रिशूलं च वरदं करसंस्थितम्।
नीलवस्त्रधरं शान्तं शनिं वन्दे दयानिधिम्॥

॥ अथ शनि सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

शनैश्चरः शनिर्मन्दः छायापुत्रो रविप्रियः।
कृष्णो नीलाम्बरधरो यमाग्रजो यमानुजः॥ १॥

सूर्यपुत्रो दीर्घदेहो विशालाक्षो गृध्रवाहनः।
मकरस्थो कुम्भपतिर्नक्षत्रेशो ग्रहोत्तमः॥ २॥

क्रूरदृष्टिर्दण्डकर्ता न्यायकृत् कर्मसाक्षिवान्।
सत्त्वधीमान् गम्भीरश्च कालस्वरूपो ह्यनन्तकः॥ ३॥

सप्तमाष्टमसंस्थानो द्वादशस्थो भयङ्करः।
पञ्चमस्थानतापी च जन्मस्थो दुःखदायकः॥ ४॥

एकादशे सुखदश्च तृतीये लाभदायकः।
षष्ठस्थो रोगनाशी च दशमे कर्मवर्धनः॥ ५॥

साढेसाती विभीषणो कण्टकशनिर्भयावहः।
ढैयाशनिमहाक्रूरो दशाप्रबलकारकः॥ ६॥

वैराग्यप्रदकर्ता च संन्यासमार्गदर्शकः।
तपस्वी योगसाधी च कर्मयोगप्रवर्तकः॥ ७॥

लोहप्रिय एव च नीलमणिप्रभाकरः।
तिलतैलप्रियो देवो कृष्णधातुविभूषितः॥ ८॥

शनिवारेश्वरो मान्यो शनिमन्त्रजपप्रियः।
छायादेवीसुतो भक्तो रविवंशप्रदीपकः॥ ९॥

कर्मफलदो नित्यं पापनाशी शुभप्रदः।
सत्यव्रतो धर्मपालो लोकरक्षी दयानिधिः॥ १०॥

दानप्रिय एव च श्रमजीविवत्सलः।
दरिद्रनाशनो दाता भक्तानुग्रहकारकः॥ ११॥

दण्डनीतिधरश्चैव पापिनां दण्डदायकः।
सज्जनानां रक्षकश्च दुर्जनानां निग्रहः॥ १२॥

सेवकप्रियदेवश्च जनसेवाफलप्रदः।
कठिनकर्मदश्चैव धैर्यबुद्धिविवर्धनः॥ १३॥

वायुपुत्रसमानश्च हनुमद्भक्तवत्सलः।
शिवभक्तिप्रदो मान्यो विष्णुभक्तिविवर्धनः॥ १४॥

पिप्पलवृक्षवासी च शमीवृक्षप्रियो ग्रहः।
काकवाहनसम्पन्नो गृध्रवाहो महाग्रहः॥ १५॥

मन्दगतिर्मन्दवीर्यो मन्दहासो मनोहरः।
संवत्सरचारी वै शनैर्गच्छन् शनैश्चरः॥ १६॥

चित्रगुप्तसहायी च यमलोकसहायकः।
पापलेखकर्ता च कर्मलेखसमर्पकः॥ १७॥

अयोध्यानाथभक्तश्च हनुमद्दासवत्सलः।
राजराजेश्वरो मान्यो दण्डकारण्यनायकः॥ १८॥

सत्कर्मसाधकश्चैव दुष्कर्मनाशनो ग्रहः।
पापक्षयकरो मान्यो पुण्यवृद्धिकरो बली॥ १९॥

सर्वग्रहप्रभावी च शनिदेवो महाबलः।
सर्वभक्तवरप्रदो सर्वसौख्यविवर्धनः॥ २०॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥

शनिसहस्रनाम्नां तु पठनाद्यत्फलं लभेत्।
साढेसातीभयं नश्येद् ढैयाशनिभयं हरेत्॥

शनिवारे तु यः पठेन्नियमेन समाहितः।
शनिदोषविनिर्मुक्तो सर्वदुःखात् प्रमुच्यते॥

कण्टकशनिभयं नश्येत् शनिदशाशुभं हरेत्।
षण्मासं पठनाद्भक्त्या सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री स्कन्दपुराणोक्तं श्री शनि सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shani Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||

Nilanjana-samabhasam Ravi-putram Yama-agrajam |
Chaya-Martanda-sambhutam tam namami Shanaishcharam ||

Krishna-varnam Chatur-bahum Gridhra-vahanam-arudham |
Dhanur-banam Trishulam cha Varadam kara-samsthitam |
Nila-vastra-dharam Shantam Shanim vande Daya-nidhim ||

|| Atha Shani Sahasranama Stotram ||

Shanaishcharah Shanir-Mandah Chaya-putro Ravi-priyah |
Krishno Nila-ambara-dharo Yama-agrajo Yama-anujah || 1 ||

Surya-putro Dirgha-deho Vishalaksho Gridhra-vahanah |
Makara-stho Kumbha-patir-Nakshatra-isho Graha-uttamah || 2 ||

Krura-drishtir-Danda-karta Nyaya-krit Karma-sakshivan |
Sattva-dhiman Gambhirash-cha Kala-svarupo hy-anantakah || 3 ||

Saptama-ashtama-samsthano Dvadasha-stho Bhayamkarah |
Panchama-sthana-tapi cha Janma-stho Duhkha-dayakah || 4 ||

Ekadashe Sukha-dash-cha Tritiye Labha-dayakah |
Shashtha-stho Roga-nashi cha Dashame Karma-vardhanah || 5 ||

Sadhesati Vibhishaano Kantaka-Shanir-bhaya-avahah |
Dhaiya-shani-maha-kruro Dasha-prabala-karakah || 6 ||

Vairagya-prada-karta cha Sannyasa-marga-darshakah |
Tapasvi Yoga-sadhi cha Karma-yoga-pravartakah || 7 ||

Loha-priya eva cha Nila-mani-prabha-karah |
Tila-taila-priyo Devo Krishna-dhatu-vibhushitah || 8 ||

Shanivara-ishwaro Manyo Shani-mantra-japa-priyah |
Chaya-devi-suto Bhakto Ravi-vamsha-pradipakah || 9 ||

Karma-phala-do nityam Papa-nashi Shubha-pradah |
Satya-vrato Dharma-palo Loka-rakshi Daya-nidhih || 10 ||

Dana-priya eva cha Shrama-jivi-vatsalah |
Daridra-nashano Data Bhakta-anugraha-karakah || 11 ||

Danda-niti-dharash-chaiva Papinam Danda-dayakah |
Sajjananam Rakshakash-cha Durjananam Nigrahah || 12 ||

Sevaka-priya-devash-cha Jana-seva-phala-pradah |
Kathina-karma-dash-chaiva Dhairya-buddhi-vivardhanah || 13 ||

Vayu-putra-samanash-cha Hanumad-bhakta-vatsalah |
Shiva-bhakti-prado Manyo Vishnu-bhakti-vivardhanah || 14 ||

Pippala-vriksha-vasi cha Shami-vriksha-priyo Grahah |
Kaka-vahana-sampanno Gridhra-vaho Maha-grahah || 15 ||

Manda-gatir-Manda-viryo Manda-haso Manoharah |
Samvatsara-chari vai Shanair-gachchhan Shanaishcharah || 16 ||

Chitragupta-sahayi cha Yama-loka-sahayakah |
Papa-lekha-karta cha Karma-lekha-samarpakah || 17 ||

Ayodhya-natha-bhaktash-cha Hanumad-dasa-vatsalah |
Raja-rajeshwaro Manyo Dandakaranya-nayakah || 18 ||

Sat-karma-sadhakash-chaiva Dush-karma-nashano Grahah |
Papa-kshaya-karo Manyo Punya-vriddhi-karo Bali || 19 ||

Sarva-graha-prabhavi cha Shani-Devo Maha-balah |
Sarva-bhakta-vara-prado Sarva-saukhya-vivardhanah || 20 ||

|| Phalashruti ||

Shani-Sahasra-namnam tu pathanad-yat-phalam labhet |
Sadhesati-bhayam nashyed Dhaiya-shani-bhayam haret ||

Shanivare tu yah pathen-niyamena samahitah |
Shani-dosha-vinirmuktah Sarva-duhkhat pramuchyate ||

Kantaka-Shani-bhayam nashyet Shani-dasha-ashubham haret |
Shan-masam pathanad-bhaktya Sarva-siddhim-avapnuyat ||

|| Iti Shri Skanda-Purana-uktam Shri Shani Sahasranama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: नीलांजन समान कान्ति, रविपुत्र, यमाग्रज, छायामार्तण्ड सम्भूत शनैश्चर को नमन। कृष्णवर्ण, चतुर्बाहु, गृध्रवाहन, धनुर्बाण-त्रिशूल-वरद हस्त, नीलवस्त्रधर, शान्त, दयानिधि शनि को वन्दन। शनैश्चर, शनि, मन्द, छायापुत्र, कृष्ण, यमाग्रज, सूर्यपुत्र, मकर-कुम्भ पति — ये शनि के प्रमुख नाम हैं। सप्तम-अष्टम स्थान पर भयंकर, एकादश में सुखद, तृतीय में लाभदायक, षष्ठ में रोगनाशी, दशम में कर्मवर्धक शनि। साढ़ेसाती, ढैय्या, कंटक शनि — ये दशाएँ कष्टकारक हैं। कर्मफलद, न्यायकृत, दण्डकर्ता, भक्तानुग्रहकारक शनि देव। शनिवार को पठन से शनिदोष, साढ़ेसाती भय, कंटक शनि भय नष्ट होता है और सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of shani dosha, sadhesati, and kantaka shani effects
Traditionally recited for removal of fear and suffering caused by saturn transits
Invokes patience, endurance, and spiritual discipline
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and grants stable prosperity
Invokes the blessing of karmic purification and righteous conduct
Six months of recitation grants all siddhis
description: Shani Sahasranama Stotram (श्री शनि सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्) is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names dedicated to Lord Shani Dev. Attributed to Skanda Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow karmic purification and righteous conduct and grant patience, endurance, and spiritual discipline. It is also said to remove poverty and grant stable prosperity. This sacred text is ideally recited on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Shani Jayanti and Shani Amavasya for enhanced blessings. With 23 verses and a reading time of about 25 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Shani Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shani Sahasranama in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. 1000 names of Saturn for Sadhesati relief and Shani dosha remedy.

seoFaqs.question: From which scripture is the Shani Sahasranama Stotram drawn?
seoFaqs.answer: The most widely circulated Shani Sahasranama Stotram is preserved in the Skanda Purana within sections devoted to Navagraha worship. It enumerates one thousand names of Lord Shanaishchara, the slow-moving son of Surya and Chhaya, embodying the planetary force of Saturn. Additional Shani namavali traditions appear in the Brahmanda Purana and in compilation texts like the Navagraha Stotra Mala used by South Indian temple priests. The Skanda Purana version remains the standard recension circulated in classical tradition and printed by Gita Press, Stotra Nidhi and major regional publishers across India. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia Shani; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Shani Sahasranama particularly recommended during Sade Sati and Dhaiya?
seoFaqs.answer: Sade Sati is the seven-and-a-half-year transit of Shani over the natal moon and the houses immediately before and after, while Dhaiya is the two-and-a-half-year transit of Shani over the fourth or eighth house from the natal moon. Both are considered karmically intense periods. The Shani Sahasranama is specifically recommended because each of its 1000 names addresses a different aspect of Shani's nature — Krishna, Mandara, Sourih, Pingala, Babhru, Roudra, Yama, Konastha — allowing the devotee to invoke his benevolent rather than punitive face. Regular recitation during these periods is believed to soften the karmic impact, accelerate dharmic learning, and prevent the worst material setbacks. (Sources: Hindupedia Shani; Drikpanchang Sade Sati; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite Shani Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Saturday (Shanivar) is the weekday belonging to Lord Shani and is the universally accepted day for the sahasranama. Within Saturday the most auspicious time is the second hour after sunrise (Shani-hora) and the early evening sandhya. Shani Jayanti, falling on Vaishakha Amavasya, and Shani Amavasya, when an Amavasya falls on a Saturday, are the supreme annual recitation days. Other recommended occasions include the start and end of Sade Sati and Dhaiya periods, Saturn transits between rashis, Saturn retrograde periods, and any morning when nightmares, accidents or unexplained illnesses suggest Shani affliction. (Sources: Drikpanchang Shani Jayanti; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Shani Sahasranama phalashruti claim?
seoFaqs.answer: The Skanda Purana phalashruti promises that recitation of all 1000 names brings the favour of Shani Dev, mitigating the effects of Sade Sati and Dhaiya, removing graha-pida (planetary affliction), preventing accidents and untimely death, restoring lost wealth and employment, healing chronic illnesses associated with Saturn (joint and bone disease, paralysis, depression), and granting longevity and steady karmic progress. It also promises the destruction of debts, the return of estranged children, and the eventual attainment of moksha through the disciplined burning of past karmas. The phalashruti specifically notes that even a single recitation on Shani Jayanti equals the merit of 108 ordinary recitations. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Vedadhara; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Shani Sahasranama different from the Dasharatha-krita Shani Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dasharatha-krita Shani Stotra is a short ten-verse hymn composed by King Dasharatha of Ayodhya when Shani was about to enter Rohini nakshatra; legend says Dasharatha confronted Shani in the heavens and pleased him with this stotra, gaining a boon that Shani would spare the kingdom. It is concise, memorable and ideal for daily recitation. The Shani Sahasranama is the much longer thousand-name compendium suited for Saturday vrats, Shani Jayanti, Sade Sati periods and serious Saturn-related remedial worship. Devotees often combine both — the Dasharatha stotra daily and the sahasranama on Saturdays. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What offerings traditionally accompany the Shani Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional offerings to Shani during the sahasranama recitation include sesame oil (til tel) for the lamp, black sesame seeds, urad dal, black cloth, blue or black flowers (especially Shami, Aparajita), iron items, mustard oil, and naivedya of til-laddu or khichdi made of urad dal. Devotees light a four-wicked or chaturmukha lamp with sesame oil before the murti or image of Shani, often at a Shani temple or Shaneshwara shrine, and circumambulate seven and a half times to symbolise Sade Sati. Offering food to crows (Shani's vahana) and helping the elderly, disabled or labouring poor on Saturdays are equally important non-ritual components. (Sources: Drikpanchang Shanivar; Hindupedia; Sanatan Sanstha.)

seoFaqs.question: Is Shani worship dangerous if performed incorrectly?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional commentaries advise reverence and consistency rather than caution. Shani is the dharma-raja and karma-phaladata — the dispenser of karmic results — and is described as fair rather than malevolent. Incorrect pronunciation of mantras carries the same general caveats as for any Sanskrit recitation and is mitigated by sincere bhakti. Householders are warned against approaching Shani only in fear or transactional bargaining; the sahasranama is most effective when recited with surrender, ethical conduct, charity to the underprivileged on Saturdays, and acceptance of his role as karmic teacher. There are no special tantric restrictions on the sahasranama itself, which is a Pauranika praise text open to all. (Sources: Hindupedia; Sanatan Sanstha; Drikpanchang.)
